title: PostgreSQL RLS infrastructure with tenant context per connection

story.i_want_to: implement PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) policies that enforce tenant isolation at the database level, with SET LOCAL for tenant context per connection and Ecto Repo callbacks that automatically set the tenant on every query
story.so_that: even if application-level scoping has a bug, the database itself prevents any cross-tenant data leakage as a defense-in-depth layer

rationale: Multi-tenant isolation is the most critical security property of loopctl. Application-level WHERE tenant_id = ? clauses are the first line of defense, but a single missed scope in any query could leak data across tenants. PostgreSQL RLS provides a database-level guarantee that is independent of application code. This is non-negotiable for a system that stores project data for multiple organizations.

acceptance_criteria.description: A migration creates a reusable SQL function set_tenant(uuid) that calls SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id = <id> for the current transaction
A migration template/helper exists to enable RLS on any table with a tenant_id column and create a policy that restricts rows to current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::uuid = tenant_id
A Loopctl.Repo.put_tenant_id/1 function stores the tenant_id in the process dictionary for use by Repo callbacks
Repo's default_options/1 or prepare_query/3 callback automatically executes SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id before every query when a tenant_id is set in the process dictionary
Queries executed without a tenant context (no tenant_id in process dictionary) are blocked by RLS and return zero rows for tenant-scoped tables (not an error, just empty results)
A superadmin bypass mechanism exists: when the process dictionary contains {:rls_bypass, true}, RLS is disabled for that connection via SET LOCAL role to a superuser role or by skipping the SET LOCAL
RLS policies are tested with at least two tenants to confirm cross-tenant isolation: tenant A cannot see tenant B's data
The tenants table itself is exempt from tenant-scoped RLS (it has no tenant_id column)
All RLS infrastructure is idempotent — running migrations multiple times does not produce errors

The application MUST configure two database connection pools: (1) Loopctl.Repo — connects as the standard role with RLS enforced, used for all tenant-scoped requests. (2) Loopctl.AdminRepo — connects as the loopctl_admin role with BYPASSRLS privilege, used ONLY for superadmin cross-tenant queries (US-11.1, US-11.3, US-11.4). Both repos share the same database but use different PostgreSQL roles.

The auth pipeline MUST route requests to the correct Repo based on the API key role. Superadmin requests (without impersonation) use AdminRepo. Impersonated requests use the standard Repo with SET LOCAL for the target tenant. Non-superadmin requests always use the standard Repo.

test_cases.name: SET LOCAL sets tenant context for current transaction
preconditions: Database is migrated with RLS functions
expected_results: current_setting returns the tenant_a_id that was set

test_cases.name: RLS prevents cross-tenant data access
preconditions: RLS-enabled test table exists with tenant_id column
steps: Set tenant context to tenant_a_id via Repo.put_tenant_id/1
Query all records from the test table
expected_results: Only records belonging to tenant_a are returned
Records belonging to tenant_b are not visible

test_cases.name: Query without tenant context returns empty results
steps: Ensure no tenant_id is set in process dictionary
Query all records from the test table

test_cases.name: Superadmin bypass sees all tenant data
steps: Query all records from the test table
expected_results: Records from both tenant_a and tenant_b are returned

test_cases.name: Repo.put_tenant_id/1 stores tenant in process dictionary
steps: Read the process dictionary for the tenant key
expected_results: The tenant_id is stored and retrievable from the process dictionary

test_cases.name: RLS policy creation is idempotent
preconditions: RLS migration has been run once
steps: Run the RLS migration a second time (rollback + migrate or re-run)

test_cases.name: Tenant context is isolated per process
steps: In process 1, set tenant context to tenant_a_id and query
In process 2 (Task.async), set tenant context to tenant_b_id and query
expected_results: Neither process leaks data to the other

technical_notes: Use SET LOCAL (not SET) so the tenant context is scoped to the current transaction and auto-resets when the connection returns to the pool
RLS policy SQL: CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON <table> USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::uuid)
Must ALTER TABLE <table> ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY and ALTER TABLE <table> FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY for the policies to apply even to table owners
The Ecto Sandbox in test mode wraps each test in a transaction, which works well with SET LOCAL

SET LOCAL requires an active transaction. Implementation options: (1) Use a Repo.transaction wrapper that executes SET LOCAL via Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query!/3 at transaction start, or (2) Use Postgrex after_connect callback. Option 1 is preferred because it's per-request, not per-connection. Note: prepare_query/3 CANNOT execute arbitrary SQL — it can only modify the Ecto query struct.

Superadmin RLS bypass: Use a separate PostgreSQL database role (e.g., loopctl_admin) that has BYPASSRLS privilege. The application connects with this role only for superadmin requests. This is cleaner than sentinel UUID approaches which require special policy clauses on every table.
IMPORTANT: The Ecto.Repo sandbox (SQL.Sandbox) used in tests already wraps queries in transactions, so SET LOCAL works naturally in test mode
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files

Two Ecto Repos: `Loopctl.Repo` (pool_size: 10, RLS enforced) and `Loopctl.AdminRepo` (pool_size: 3, BYPASSRLS). Oban must use the standard Repo. The Ecto sandbox in tests must allow both repos — configure sandbox for both in test_helper.exs.
